methods.description: Mesowear analysis is a method of evaluating the diet based on the relative amounts of attritive and abrasive dental wear. It categorizes the gross dental wear of ungulate molars by evaluating the relief and sharpness of cusp apices.

steps.description: Mesowear is scored macroscopically from the buccal side of the upper M2.
steps.searchable_summary: score mesowear macroscopically from the buccal side of the upper M2

steps.description: The method used here is based on seven cusp categories (numbered from 0 to 6), ranging in shape from high and sharp to completely blunt with no relief.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize cusp shapes into seven categories

steps.description: The average value of the mesowear data corresponds to the 'mesowear score' or MWS.

methods.description: The microwear analysis was performed on the enamel surfaces of the occlusal surfaces using a stereomicroscope on high-resolution epoxy casts of teeth. It quantifies microwear features such as pits and scratches to infer dietary habits.

steps.description: The occlusal surface of each specimen was cleaned using acetone followed by 96% ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: clean occlusal surface with acetone and ethanol

steps.description: The full occlusal surface was molded using high-resolution silicone, and casts were created using clear epoxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: create epoxy resin casts from silicone molds

steps.description: Casts were screened under a stereomicroscope, and those with poorly preserved enamel or taphonomic defects were removed.
steps.searchable_summary: screen casts under stereomicroscope and remove defective ones

steps.description: Microwear features were quantified on the paracone of the upper M2 using an ocular reticule.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify microwear features on paracone using ocular reticule

steps.description: Scratches and pits were quantified in four areas on the enamel surface and averaged to obtain a single value per specimen.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify and average scratches and pits
